data.title: Asia Markets Mixed, Europe Slips On Auto Tariff Worries, Gold Advances Toward Record High - Global Markets Today While US Slept
BlackRock's $19 Biliion Port Deal With Li Ka-shing Sparks China Scrutiny, State Firms Told To Pause New Ties: Report
data.description: Li Ka-shing's business empire faces scrutiny from China after his company CK Hutchison announced a landmark $19 billion port sale.

data.title: GM, Ford, Tesla And Other Auto Stocks Slide In Thursday Pre-Market: What's Going On?
data.description: The stocks of automakers, Ford Inc. (NYSE: F), Stellantis NV (NYSE: STLA) and Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA) declined 3.69%, 2.58%, and 1.59%, respectively during the pre-market trading session on Thursday.

data.title: US Stocks Likely To Open Mixed: 'Expect Volatility. Buckle Up,' Says Expert
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were swinging between gains and losses on Thursday after snapping a three-day winning streak on Wednesday. Futures of major benchmark indices were mixed in premarket trading.



Aiming to boost U.S. manufacturing and reduce reliance on global suppliers, new auto tariffs were introduced on Wednesday, which will go into effect from April 3.

data.title: After Siri Leadership Change, Apple Makes Another Major Executive Move in Retail: Report
data.description: Apple is reportedly promoting longtime executive Vanessa Trigub to a newly created retail leadership role, signaling succession planning for senior VP Deirdre O'Brien as part of a broader leadership reshuffle.
